verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचगोवर्धने धृते शैले आसाराद् रक्षिते व्रजे । गोलोकादाव्रजत्कृष्णं सुरभि: शक्र एव च ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācagovardhane dhṛte śaileāsārād rakṣite vrajego-lokād āvrajat kṛṣṇaṁsurabhiḥ śakra eva ca
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: After Kṛṣṇa had lifted Govardhana Hill and thus protected the inhabitants of Vraja from the terrible rainfall, Surabhi, the mother of the cows, came from her planet to see Kṛṣṇa. She was accompanied by Indra.

verses.sanskrit: विविक्त उपसङ्गम्य व्रीडीत: कृतहेलन: । पस्पर्श पादयोरेनं किरीटेनार्कवर्चसा ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: vivikta upasaṅgamyavrīḍītaḥ kṛta-helanaḥpasparśa pādayor enaṁkirīṭenārka-varcasā
verses.text: Indra was very ashamed of having offended the Lord. Approaching Him in a solitary place, Indra fell down and lay his helmet, whose effulgence was as brilliant as the sun, upon the Lord’s lotus feet.

verses.sanskrit: द‍ृष्टश्रुतानुभावोऽस्य कृष्णस्यामिततेजस: । नष्टत्रिलोकेशमद इदमाह कृताञ्जलि: ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: dṛṣṭa-śrutānubhāvo ’syakṛṣṇasyāmita-tejasaḥnaṣṭa-tri-lokeśa-madaidam āha kṛtāñjaliḥ
verses.text: Indra had now heard of and seen the transcendental power of omnipotent Kṛṣṇa, and his false pride in being the lord of the three worlds was thus defeated. Holding his hands together in supplication, he addressed the Lord as follows.

verses.sanskrit: इन्द्र उवाचविशुद्धसत्त्वं तव धाम शान्तंतपोमयं ध्वस्तरजस्तमस्कम् । मायामयोऽयं गुणसम्प्रवाहोन विद्यते तेऽग्रहणानुबन्ध: ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: indra uvācaviśuddha-sattvaṁ tava dhāma śāntaṁtapo-mayaṁ dhvasta-rajas-tamaskammāyā-mayo ’yaṁ guṇa-sampravāhona vidyate te grahaṇānubandhaḥ
verses.text: King Indra said: Your transcendental form, a manifestation of pure goodness, is undisturbed by change, shining with knowledge and devoid of passion and ignorance. In You does not exist the mighty flow of the modes of material nature, which is based on illusion and ignorance.

verses.sanskrit: कुतो नु तद्धेतव ईश तत्कृतालोभादयो येऽबुधलिङ्गभावा: । तथापि दण्डं भगवान् बिभर्तिधर्मस्य गुप्‍त्यै लनिग्रहाय ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: kuto nu tad-dhetava īśa tat-kṛtālobhādayo ye ’budha-linga-bhāvāḥtathāpi daṇḍaṁ bhagavān bibhartidharmasya guptyai khala-nigrahāya
verses.text: How, then, could there exist in You the symptoms of an ignorant person — such as greed, lust, anger and envy — which are produced by one’s previous involvement in material existence and which cause one to become further entangled in material existence? And yet as the Supreme Lord You impose punishment to protect religious principles and curb down the wicked.

verses.sanskrit: पिता गुरुस्त्वं जगतामधीशोदुरत्यय: काल उपात्तदण्ड: । हिताय चेच्छातनुभि: समीहसेमानं विधुन्वन् जगदीशमानिनाम् ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: pitā gurus tvaṁ jagatām adhīśoduratyayaḥ kāla upātta-daṇḍaḥhitāya cecchā-tanubhiḥ samīhasemānaṁ vidhunvan jagad-īśa-māninām
verses.text: You are the father and spiritual master of this entire universe, and also its supreme controller. You are insurmountable time, imposing punishment upon the sinful for their own benefit. Indeed, in Your various incarnations, selected by Your own free will, You act decisively to remove the false pride of those who presume themselves masters of this world.

verses.sanskrit: ये मद्विधाज्ञा जगदीशमानिन-स्त्वां वीक्ष्य कालेऽभयमाशु तन्मदम् । हित्वार्यमार्गं प्रभजन्त्यपस्मयाईहा खलानामपि तेऽनुशासनम् ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: ye mad-vidhājñā jagad-īśa-māninastvāṁ vīkṣya kāle ’bhayam āśu tan-madamhitvārya-mārgaṁ prabhajanty apasmayāīhā khalānām api te ’nuśāsanam
verses.text: Even fools like me, who proudly think themselves universal lords, quickly give up their conceit and directly take to the path of the spiritually progressive when they see You are fearless even in the face of time. Thus You punish the mischievous only to instruct them.

verses.sanskrit: स त्वं ममैश्वर्यमदप्लुतस्यकृतागसस्तेऽविदुष: प्रभावम् । क्षन्तुं प्रभोऽथार्हसि मूढचेतसोमैवं पुनर्भून्मतिरीश मेऽसती ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa tvaṁ mamaiśvarya-mada-plutasyakṛtāgasas te ’viduṣaḥ prabhāvamkṣantuṁ prabho ’thārhasi mūḍha-cetasomaivaṁ punar bhūn matir īśa me ’satī
verses.text: Engrossed in pride over my ruling power, ignorant of Your majesty, I offended You. O Lord, may You forgive me. My intelligence was bewildered, but let my consciousness never again be so impure.

verses.sanskrit: तवावतारोऽयमधोक्षजेहभुवो भराणामुरुभारजन्मनाम् । चमूपतीनामभवाय देवभवाय युष्मच्चरणानुवर्तिनाम् ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tavāvatāro ’yam adhokṣajehabhuvo bharāṇām uru-bhāra-janmanāmcamū-patīnām abhavāya devabhavāya yuṣmac-caraṇānuvartinām
verses.text: You descend into this world, O transcendent Lord, to destroy the warlords who burden the earth and create many terrible disturbances. O Lord, you simultaneously act for the welfare of those who faithfully serve Your lotus feet.

verses.sanskrit: नमस्तुभ्यं भगवते पुरुषाय महात्मने । वासुदेवाय कृष्णाय सात्वतां पतये नम: ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: namas tubhyaṁ bhagavatepuruṣāya mahātmanevāsudevāya kṛṣṇāyasātvatāṁ pataye namaḥ
verses.text: Obeisances unto You, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, the great Soul, who are all-pervading and who reside in the hearts of all. My obeisances unto You, Kṛṣṇa, the chief of the Yadu dynasty.

verses.sanskrit: स्वच्छन्दोपात्तदेहाय विशुद्धज्ञानमूर्तये । सर्वस्मै सर्वबीजाय सर्वभूतात्मने नम: ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: svacchandopātta-dehāyaviśuddha-jñāna-mūrtayesarvasmai sarva-bījāyasarva-bhūtātmane namaḥ
verses.text: Unto Him who assumes transcendental bodies according to the desires of His devotees, unto Him whose form is itself pure consciousness, unto Him who is everything, who is the seed of everything and who is the Soul of all creatures, I offer my obeisances.

verses.sanskrit: मयेदं भगवन् गोष्ठनाशायासारवायुभि: । चेष्टितं विहते यज्ञे मानिना तीव्रमन्युना ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: mayedaṁ bhagavan goṣṭha-nāśāyāsāra-vāyubhiḥceṣṭitaṁ vihate yajñemāninā tīvra-manyunā
verses.text: My dear Lord, when my sacrifice was disrupted I became fiercely angry because of false pride. Thus I tried to destroy Your cowherd community with severe rain and wind.

verses.sanskrit: त्वयेशानुगृहीतोऽस्मि ध्वस्तस्तम्भो वृथोद्यम: । ईश्वरं गुरुमात्मानं त्वामहं शरणं गत: ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tvayeśānugṛhīto ’smidhvasta-stambho vṛthodyamaḥīśvaraṁ gurum ātmānaṁtvām ahaṁ śaraṇaṁ gataḥ
verses.text: O Lord, You have shown mercy to me by shattering my false pride and defeating my attempt [to punish Vṛndāvana]. To You, the Supreme Lord, spiritual master and Supreme Soul, I have now come for shelter.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचएवं सङ्कीर्तित: कृष्णो मघोना भगवानमुम् । मेघगम्भीरया वाचा प्रहसन्निदमब्रवीत् ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaevaṁ saṅkīrtitaḥ kṛṣṇomaghonā bhagavān amummegha-gambhīrayā vācāprahasann idam abravīt
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Thus glorified by Indra, Lord Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, smiled and then spoke to him as follows in a voice resonant like the clouds.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाचमया तेऽकारि मघवन् मखभङ्गोऽनुगृह्णता । मदनुस्मृतये नित्यं मत्तस्येन्द्र श्रिया भृशम् ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvācamayā te ’kāri maghavanmakha-bhaṅgo ’nugṛhṇatāmad-anusmṛtaye nityaṁmattasyendra-śriyā bhṛśam
verses.text: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: My dear Indra, it was out of mercy that I stopped the sacrifice meant for you. You were greatly intoxicated by your opulence as King of heaven, and I wanted you to always remember Me.

verses.sanskrit: मामैश्वर्यश्रीमदान्धो दण्डपाणिं न पश्यति । तं भ्रंशयामि सम्पद्‌भ्यो यस्य चेच्छाम्यनुग्रहम् ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: mām aiśvarya-śrī-madāndhodaṇḍa pāṇiṁ na paśyatitaṁ bhraṁśayāmi sampadbhyoyasya cecchāmy anugraham
verses.text: A man blinded by intoxication with his power and opulence cannot see Me nearby with the rod of punishment in My hand. If I desire his real welfare, I drag him down from his materially fortunate position.

verses.sanskrit: गम्यतां शक्र भद्रं व: क्रियतां मेऽनुशासनम् । स्थीयतां स्वाधिकारेषु युक्तैर्व: स्तम्भवर्जितै: ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: gamyatāṁ śakra bhadraṁ vaḥkriyatāṁ me ’nuśāsanamsthīyatāṁ svādhikāreṣuyuktair vaḥ stambha-varjitaiḥ
verses.text: Indra, you may now go. Execute My order and remain in your appointed position as King of heaven. But be sober, without false pride.

verses.sanskrit: अथाह सुरभि: कृष्णमभिवन्द्यमनस्विनी । स्वसन्तानैरुपामन्‍त्र्य गोपरूपिणमीश्वरम् ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: athāha surabhiḥ kṛṣṇamabhivandya manasvinīsva-santānair upāmantryagopa-rūpiṇam īśvaram
verses.text: Mother Surabhi, along with her progeny, the cows, then offered her obeisances to Lord Kṛṣṇa. Respectfully requesting His attention, the gentle lady addressed the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who was present before her as a cowherd boy.

verses.sanskrit: सुरभिरुवाचकृष्ण कृष्ण महायोगिन् विश्वात्मन् विश्वसम्भव । भवता लोकनाथेन सनाथा वयमच्युत ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: surabhir uvācakṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa mahā-yoginviśvātman viśva-sambhavabhavatā loka-nāthenasa-nāthā vayam acyuta
verses.text: Mother Surabhi said: O Kṛṣṇa, Kṛṣṇa, greatest of mystics! O Soul and origin of the universe! You are the master of the world, and by Your grace, O infallible Lord, we have You as our master.

verses.sanskrit: त्वं न: परमकं दैवं त्वं न इन्द्रो जगत्पते । भवाय भव गोविप्रदेवानां ये च साधव: ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: tvaṁ naḥ paramakaṁ daivaṁtvaṁ na indro jagat-patebhavāya bhava go-vipradevānāṁ ye ca sādhavaḥ
verses.text: You are our worshipable Deity. Therefore, O Lord of the universe, for the benefit of the cows, the brāhmaṇas, the demigods and all other saintly persons, please become our Indra.

verses.sanskrit: इन्द्रं नस्त्वाभिषेक्ष्यामो ब्रह्मणा चोदिता वयम् । अवतीर्णोऽसि विश्वात्मन् भूमेर्भारापनुत्तये ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: indraṁ nas tvābhiṣekṣyāmobrahmaṇā coditā vayamavatīrṇo ’si viśvātmanbhūmer bhārāpanuttaye
verses.text: As ordered by Lord Brahmā, we shall perform Your bathing ceremony to coronate You as Indra. O Soul of the universe, You descend to this world to relieve the burden of the earth.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचएवं कृष्णमुपामन्‍त्र्य सुरभि: पयसात्मन: । जलैराकाशगङ्गाया ऐरावतकरोद्‌धृतै: ॥ २२ ॥ इन्द्र: सुरर्षिभि: साकं चोदितो देवमातृभि: । अभ्यसिञ्चत दाशार्हं गोविन्द इति चाभ्यधात् ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: śṛī-śuka uvācaevaṁ kṛṣṇam upāmantryasurabhiḥ payasātmanaḥjalair ākāśa-gaṅgāyāairāvata-karoddhṛtaiḥ
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Having thus appealed to Lord Kṛṣṇa, mother Surabhi performed His bathing ceremony with her own milk, and Indra, ordered by Aditi and other mothers of the demigods, anointed the Lord with heavenly Gaṅgā water from the trunk of Indra’s elephant carrier, Airāvata. Thus, in the company of the demigods and great sages, Indra coronated Lord Kṛṣṇa, the descendant of Daśārha, and gave Him the name Govinda.

verses.sanskrit: तत्रागतास्तुम्बुरुनारदादयोगन्धर्वविद्याधरसिद्धचारणा: । जगुर्यशो लोकमलापहं हरे:सुराङ्गना: सन्ननृतुर्मुदान्विता: ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatrāgatās tumburu-nāradādayogandharva-vidyādhara-siddha-cāraṇāḥjagur yaśo loka-malāpahaṁ hareḥsurāṅganāḥ sannanṛtur mudānvitāḥ
verses.text: Tumburu, Nārada and other Gandharvas, along with the Vidyādharas, Siddhas and Cāraṇas, came there to sing the glories of Lord Hari, which purify the entire world. And the wives of the demigods, filled with joy, danced together in the Lord’s honor.

verses.sanskrit: तं तुष्टुवुर्देवनिकायकेतवोह्यवाकिरंश्चाद्भुतपुष्पवृष्टिभि: । लोका: परां निर्वृतिमाप्नुवंस्त्रयोगावस्तदा गामनयन् पयोद्रुताम् ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ tuṣṭuvur deva-nikāya-ketavohy avākiraṁś cādbhuta-puṣpa-vṛṣṭibhiḥlokāḥ parāṁ nirvṛtim āpnuvaṁs trayogāvas tadā gām anayan payo-drutām
verses.text: The most eminent demigods chanted the praises of the Lord and scattered wonderful showers of flowers all around Him. All three worlds felt supreme satisfaction, and the cows drenched the surface of the earth with their milk.

verses.sanskrit: नानारसौघा: सरितो वृक्षा आसन् मधुस्रवा: । अकृष्टपच्यौषधयो गिरयोऽबिभ्रनुन्मणीन् ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: nānā-rasaughāḥ saritovṛkṣā āsan madhu-sravāḥakṛṣṭa-pacyauṣadhayogirayo ’bibhran un maṇīn
verses.text: Rivers flowed with various kinds of tasty liquids, trees exuded honey, edible plants came to maturity without cultivation, and hills gave forth jewels formerly hidden in their interiors.

verses.sanskrit: कृष्णेऽभिषिक्त एतानि सर्वाणि कुरुनन्दन । निर्वैराण्यभवंस्तात क्रूराण्यपि निसर्गत: ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: kṛṣṇe ’bhiṣikta etānisarvāṇi kuru-nandananirvairāṇy abhavaṁs tātakrūrāṇy api nisargataḥ
verses.text: O Parīkṣit, beloved of the Kuru dynasty, upon the ceremonial bathing of Lord Kṛṣṇa, all living creatures, even those cruel by nature, became entirely free of enmity.

verses.sanskrit: इति गोगोकुलपतिं गोविन्दमभिषिच्य स: । अनुज्ञातो ययौ शक्रो वृतो देवादिभिर्दिवम् ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: iti go-gokula-patiṁgovindam abhiṣicya saḥanujñāto yayau śakrovṛto devādibhir divam
verses.text: After he had ceremonially bathed Lord Govinda, who is the master of the cows and the cowherd community, King Indra took the Lord’s permission and, surrounded by the demigods and other higher beings, returned to his heavenly abode.
